food_subgroup: Hot and cold malted chocolate beverages

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Milo cold drink as served in Singapore hawker or restaurant context; closest parent 'chocolate malt beverage' (FOODON_03301710) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP for chocolate-flavoured drink used as closest parent; no specific Milo Singapore cold-serve code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226365003 'Chocolate drink' used as closest parent; no Milo-specific or malted-chocolate-cold-drink code exists.

preparation_method: mixed with cold water or cold milk
main_ingredients: Milo powder (malted barley extract, cocoa, sugar, skimmed milk powder, vitamins, minerals)
cold water or cold full-cream milk

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of cold Milo is approximately 250 ml (one cup or glass), prepared by dissolving Milo powder in cold water or cold milk and serving over ice, as commonly ordered at Singapore kopitiams and hawker centres.

nutrient_source.title: Milo Chocolate Malt Beverage Powder — Nestlé Singapore Nutritional Information (per 100 g powder and per prepared serving)
nutrient_source.organization: Nestlé Singapore / USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods cross-reference (NDB No. 45226764, Milo Chocolate Malt Beverage Mix)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are derived from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Milo Chocolate Malt Beverage Mix (powder form) and adapted to reflect a standard prepared cold drink at typical Singapore dilution (approximately 20 g powder in 230 ml cold water/milk to yield ~250 ml serving). Per-100 g values below represent the prepared ready-to-drink beverage at this dilution ratio. Core macros cross-checked against HPB Singapore Foodo category data for malted chocolate beverages.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; Milo chocolate malt beverage (prepared with water) GI ~55
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 55 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2021 International GI Tables (Atkinson et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2021) for Milo chocolate malt beverage prepared with water. This is classified as low GI (55 or less). Glycemic load per 250 ml serving calculated as: (55 × 26.0 g available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 14.3, rounded to 14.4. Cold preparation does not materially alter GI relative to the tested product.

hypertension.guidance: A 250 ml serving of cold Milo contributes approximately 90 mg sodium, which is modest relative to the 2,000 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals, making occasional consumption acceptable within a sodium-controlled diet.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar and low glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: Cold Milo has a glycemic index of approximately 55 (low) and a glycemic load of ~14 per 250 ml serving due to its high added-sugar content (~23 g per serving); individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size, consider sugar-free Milo variants, and account for this beverage within their daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Cold Milo prepared with water contains low saturated fat (~1.25 g per 250 ml serving) and negligible trans fat, but its added sugar content (~23 g per serving) warrants moderation for individuals managing cardiovascular risk, particularly those with dyslipidaemia or metabolic syndrome.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cold Milo (美禄冷) is a chilled malted chocolate beverage that is one of the most iconic drinks in Singapore, Malaysia, and across Southeast Asia. It is prepared by dissolving Nestlé Milo powder — a blend of malted barley extract, cocoa, sugar, skimmed milk powder, and added vitamins and minerals — in cold water or cold full-cream milk, then serving over ice. In Singapore kopitiams and hawker centres, 'Milo Peng' (冷美禄) refers specifically to the iced cold version, as opposed to 'Milo Panas' (hot Milo). The drink has a sweet, malty, lightly chocolatey flavour with a smooth, slightly creamy texture when prepared with milk. Nutritionally, a standard 250 ml serving prepared with water provides approximately 140 kcal, 4 g protein, 2.25 g fat, and 26 g carbohydrate, with meaningful contributions of calcium, iron, and B vitamins from the fortified powder. The glycemic index is approximately 55 (low GI), yielding a glycemic load of around 14 per serving, which is clinically relevant for individuals managing blood glucose. Cold Milo is vegetarian and halal-certified. It is not suitable for individuals with gluten intolerance (contains barley malt) or dairy allergy (contains milk). In Singapore food culture, Milo is deeply embedded as a childhood staple, school canteen drink, and everyday kopitiam order, and is frequently referenced in discussions of local food identity.
common_user_queries: How many calories in cold Milo?
What is the GI of Milo?
How much sugar is in cold Milo?
Is cold Milo good for high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Milo powder and calculated for the prepared ready-to-drink beverage at standard Singapore dilution. No exact per-100 g prepared-drink entry was found in HPB Singapore Foodo for this specific product; values are cross-checked against HPB malted beverage category data. GI of 55 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database / 2021 International GI Tables for Milo prepared with water, which is a direct product match. Confidence is medium rather than high because the per-100 g values reflect an adapted calculation from powder-form data rather than a direct laboratory analysis of the prepared cold drink.
notes: 1. Per-100 g nutrient values represent the prepared cold drink at approximately 20 g Milo powder dissolved in 230 ml cold water (standard Singapore kopitiam dilution), yielding ~250 ml per serving. Values will differ if prepared with full-cream milk (higher fat, protein, calcium). 2. selenium_mcg is set to -1 as selenium content is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for Milo powder nor in the Nestlé Singapore product label. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Milo powder. 4. GI of 55 sourced from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021 International GI Tables for Milo chocolate malt beverage prepared with water; cold preparation does not materially alter GI. This GI of 55 is classified as low. 5. Glycemic load per 250 ml serving = (55 × 26.0) / 100 = 14.3, reported as 14.4. 6. This record reflects the cold/iced preparation (Milo Peng); a separate record should be created for hot Milo (Milo Panas) if required. 7. Allergens: contains milk and gluten (barley malt); not suitable for lactose-intolerant or coeliac individuals. 8. Halal status: Nestlé Milo is MUIS-certified halal in Singapore.
